target.title: fix(agents): keep cli-auth-epoch stable when local credential file presence flips
target.body:
## Summary

- Extend `shouldSkipLocalCliCredentialEpoch` at the prepare/backend auth-epoch policy seam to skip local credentials for identity-less OAuth profiles (no email, no accountId).
- When a profile credential is OAuth but carries no stable account identity, local credential file presence was flipping the auth epoch hash, causing spurious `reason=auth-epoch` session resets for Anthropic Max plan users.
- Both sides encode the same identity-less constant (`["oauth","provider",null,null,null,null,null]`), so the only observable epoch change is file existence — not an actual account change.
- The fix keeps combined-mode behavior intact for identity-bearing profiles (those with email or accountId).
- The identity-less condition does NOT require `preparedExecution` because the bundled claude-cli backend does not register `prepareExecution`.

### What did NOT change

- Combined-mode epoch calculation for identity-bearing profiles (those with email/accountId) is untouched — these still include local credential fingerprints.
- The `profile-only` mode and its `preparedExecution` requirement remain unchanged.
- No changes to auth session storage, credential reading, or epoch hash algorithm.
- `CLI_AUTH_EPOCH_VERSION` bump from 4 to 5 is the only version change; no migration or data format changes.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] New feature (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] Refactoring (no functional change)
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] CI/Build change

## Scope

- [x] Agents/cli-auth-epoch
- [x] CLI/prepare
- [ ] Core messaging
- [ ] Plugins
- [ ] Channel adapters
- [ ] Configuration

## Root Cause

`shouldSkipLocalCliCredentialEpoch` in `src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.ts` only had one skip condition: `profile-only` mode with `preparedExecution` present. Identity-less OAuth profiles (e.g. Anthropic Max plan via claude-cli, which has no email/accountId) were not covered. When the local CLI credential file appeared or disappeared (e.g. during login/logout), the auth epoch hash flipped because the local credential fingerprint changed — even though both sides encode the same identity-less constant. This triggered mass `reason=auth-epoch` session resets reported in #80178.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Identity-less OAuth profiles (no email/accountId) skip local credential fingerprints in the auth epoch, preventing spurious session resets when the local credential file appears or disappears. This covers the real claude-cli backend path which has no `prepareExecution`.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, `pnpm test` + `node --import tsx` live verification.

**Steps run after the patch:**

    node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
    import { shouldSkipLocalCliCredentialEpoch } from './src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.js';
    const now = Date.now() + 60000;
    const cases = [
      { label: 'claude-cli identity-less (no preparedExecution)',
        args: { authProfileId: 'anthropic:claude-cli',
          authCredential: { type: 'oauth', provider: 'claude-cli', access: 'a', refresh: 'r', expires: now } } },
      { label: 'claude-cli identity-less (with preparedExecution)',
        args: { authProfileId: 'anthropic:claude-cli',
          authCredential: { type: 'oauth', provider: 'claude-cli', access: 'a', refresh: 'r', expires: now },
          preparedExecution: {} } },
      { label: 'identity-bearing OAuth (no preparedExecution)',
        args: { authProfileId: 'anthropic:work',
          authCredential: { type: 'oauth', provider: 'anthropic', access: 'a', refresh: 'r', expires: now,
            email: 'user@example.com' } } },
      { label: 'no auth profile selected',
        args: { authCredential: { type: 'oauth', provider: 'claude-cli', access: 'a', refresh: 'r', expires: now } } },
      { label: 'profile-only (no preparedExecution)',
        args: { authEpochMode: 'profile-only', authProfileId: 'anthropic:claude-cli',
          authCredential: { type: 'oauth', provider: 'claude-cli', access: 'a', refresh: 'r', expires: now } } },
    ];
    for (const c of cases) {
      console.log(c.label + ':', shouldSkipLocalCliCredentialEpoch(c.args));
    }
    "

**Evidence after fix:**

    claude-cli identity-less (no preparedExecution): true
    claude-cli identity-less (with preparedExecution): true
    identity-bearing OAuth (no preparedExecution): false
    no auth profile selected: false
    profile-only (no preparedExecution): false

**Observed result after fix:** The claude-cli identity-less path correctly skips local credentials without requiring `preparedExecution`; identity-bearing profiles remain in combined mode; profile-only mode still requires `preparedExecution`. All 22 prepare tests, 13 cli-auth-epoch tests, and 14 cli-session tests pass. The `CLI_AUTH_EPOCH_VERSION` bump to 5 ensures existing sessions recalculate epochs under the corrected policy.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end session reuse with live Claude CLI credentials. The policy seam fix is verified at the function level with the exact parameter combinations that appear in production.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/cli-runner/prepare.test.ts` — 22 passed
- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/cli-auth-epoch.test.ts` — 13 passed (includes new `keeps identity-less claude-cli OAuth epochs stable across local file presence (#80178)` test)
- [x] `pnpm test src/agents/cli-session.test.ts` — 14 passed
- [ ] Manual: with identity-less claude-cli profile, verify `reason=auth-epoch` no longer appears when local credential file is created/deleted

## User-visible Changes

Anthropic Max plan users using claude-cli will no longer experience mass session invalidation (`reason=auth-epoch` resets) when the local CLI credential file appears or disappears. All other auth profiles (identity-bearing OAuth, API keys) are unaffected.

## Diagram

    Before:
      shouldSkipLocalCliCredentialEpoch
        -> profile-only mode + preparedExecution? skip
        -> otherwise: include local credential fingerprint
        -> identity-less OAuth: fingerprint flips on file presence change -> epoch reset

    After:
      shouldSkipLocalCliCredentialEpoch
        -> profile-only mode + preparedExecution? skip
        -> identity-less OAuth (no email, no accountId)? skip  <-- new
        -> otherwise: include local credential fingerprint
        -> identity-less OAuth: fingerprint excluded -> epoch stable

## Security Impact

No security boundary changes. The epoch is a session invalidation signal — making it more stable for identity-less profiles reduces spurious resets without weakening invalidation for genuine credential changes. Identity-bearing profiles (with email/accountId) still include local credential fingerprints as before. The `CLI_AUTH_EPOCH_VERSION` bump to 5 triggers a one-time recalculation but does not invalidate stored sessions.

Closes #80178
